modes.name: CRC-16-CCITT
modes.description: Cyclic Redundancy Check 16-bit Consultative Commitee for International Telegraph and Telephone
notes: Used for error detection in communication and storage systems

modes.name: MySQL323
modes.characteristics: 16 chars typical, but can be padded to 32 (hexadecimals)

modes.name: CRC-96(ZIP)
modes.description: CRC-96 used in some ZIP variants

modes.name: MD5
notes: Used as checksum to verify data or file integrity
MD5 is cryptographically broken as it is vulnerable to collision attacks

modes.name: LM
modes.characteristics: 16 hex chars, all uppercase, split password
notes: Mainly found in Windows SAM files(legacy Windows)

modes.name: LM
notes: Mainly found in Windows SAM files(legacy Windows)

modes.name: NTLM
modes.characteristics: 32 chars, Windows authentication, based on MD4

modes.name: SHA-1
notes: found in git commits and legacy certificates

modes.name: Android PIN
modes.characteristics: 40 chars hash + 16 chars salt, SHA1 + MD5

modes.name: SHA-512 Crypt
notes: Industry standard for modern Linux systems
